channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nova Scotia Wildfire Grows to 1,700 Hectares, Evacuations in Place
item.description: A wildfire near Mooseland Road on Nova Scotia’s Eastern Shore has expanded to 1,700 hectares, up from an earlier estimate of 300 hectares. Mandatory evacuations remain in effect for a 22-kilometre str...

item.title: PCSO Lotto Draw on July 17, 2026: No Winners for Major Jackpots
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that there were no winners for either of the major lotto jackpots offered on Friday, July 17, 2026. The Ultra Lotto 6/58 jackpot, which had a...

item.title: Andy Burnham elected as UK prime minister amid leadership transition and policy challenges
item.description: Andy Burnham has been officially declared leader of the UK Labour Party, clearing the final hurdle to become prime minister. The former mayor of Greater Manchester secured 379 nominations from Labour ...

item.title: Wildfire Smoke from Ontario Causes Air Quality Concerns in Winnipeg
item.description: Environment and Climate Change Canada (ECCC) has issued an air quality warning for Winnipeg as southeasterly winds carry smoke from wildfires burning in northwestern Ontario. The yellow-level alert wa...

item.title: Blackwater secures second win against Magnolia in PBA game
item.description: The Philippine Basketball Association (PBA) game between Blackwater and Magnolia concluded with Blackwater emerging victorious, marking their second win in the season. The match, held on July 18, 2026...

item.title: UK Drops Foreign Power Allegations in Adil Raja and Shahzad Akbar Attack Case
item.description: The UK Prosecution Service has withdrawn allegations linking the National Security Act and foreign power provisions to the case of former accountability adviser Mirza Shahzad Akbar and YouTuber Adil R...

item.title: Zimbabwean Woman Dies at Beitbridge Border; South Africa Clarifies Not Part of Repatriation
item.description: A Zimbabwean woman who collapsed and died at the Beitbridge Border Post in Limpopo, South Africa, was not part of the official repatriation process, according to the Home Affairs Department. Chief Dir...

item.title: Claudia Winkleman leaves BBC chat show after one season
item.description: The article compiles a series of celebrity news snippets, including Claudia Winkleman’s exit from her BBC chat show, Sharon Gaffka welcoming her first child, and other entertainment-related updates. H...

item.title: Website access issue: News Corp Australia blocks crawler bot activity
item.description: The article discusses an error message encountered by users attempting to access News Corp Australia websites, indicating they may have been detected as crawler bots. News Corp Australia employs softw...

item.title: White House Teleprompter Operator Faces Insider Trading Allegations
item.description: Gabriel Perez, the White House teleprompter operator, is under investigation for allegedly using his access to presidential speeches to bet on Kalshi markets. ABC News reports that Perez reportedly ma...

item.title: Salon Owner Destroys Equipment Amid Anti-Immigration Tensions in South Africa
item.description: A video capturing a chaotic confrontation at a South African hair salon went viral, sparking widespread attention amid ongoing anti-immigration protests. The footage shows a woman in a zebra-print out...

item.title: Political leaders condemn JUI-F chief's controversial remarks on military martyrs
item.description: PML-N leader Rana Sanaullah criticized Maulana Fazlur Rehman's remarks about army martyrs, calling them 'inappropriate' and damaging to national morale. He emphasized that such comments could demotiva...

item.title: Federal Govt launches 1,500-unit housing project for immigration officers in Abuja
item.description: The Federal Government has officially launched a 1,500-unit housing project designed to improve living conditions for Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS) personnel. Located at Kutunku Extension in Gwagw...

item.title: NBI investigates 2019 SEA Games financial management and alleged irregularities
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) is conducting a probe into the management and financial aspects of the 2019 Southeast Asian Games (SEA Games), following allegations of irregularities. The i...

item.title: Homeland Security Secretary Mullin threatens legal action against states resisting Trump's election security measures
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin has openly threatened to jail state officials who refuse to cooperate with the Trump administration's midterm election security program. During a press con...

item.title: Government Revises Licence Fees for Central Govt Quarters Effective July 1
item.description: The Union ministry of housing and urban affairs has revised the licence fees for central government employees occupying General Pool Residential Accommodation (GPRA) across all categories from Type-I ...

item.title: Junior Springboks Honor Late Player as They Aim for World Title
item.description: The Junior Springboks, led by captain Siphosethu Mnebele, are set to honor the late Luqobo Makwedini during their World Rugby U20 Championship final against France. Makwedini, a former South African U...

item.title: Children from 22 African Countries Unite Through Chess Tournament
item.description: The World School Team Chess Championship Africa stage concluded in Cape Town, marking the first continent-wide tournament for school teams. Twenty-six schools from 22 nations competed, with Uganda's S...

item.title: Ruth Madeley on disability representation and her new role in 'The Rapture'
item.description: Ruth Madeley, a British actress and disability campaigner, discusses her role in the BBC series 'The Rapture' and her journey in the entertainment industry. The show, an adaptation of Liz Jensen's nov...

item.title: CN Railway Workers Escape Wildfires on Foot as Regulators Investigate Incident
item.description: Federal regulators are investigating why Canadian National Railway (CN) workers had to flee wildfires on foot in Northwestern Ontario. A CN train crew was forced to abandon their locomotive when it wa...
